description_long: This shrub consists of a linear arrangement of six identical, low-growing leafy plants, each with broad, rounded green leaves that radiate outward from a central stem. The leaves display a mottled pattern of lighter and darker green, suggesting a healthy, robust foliage. The plants are spaced evenly along a straight axis, forming a continuous row that could serve as a border or edging in an urban landscape. There are no visible flowers or fruits, and the stems are short, keeping the overall profile close to the ground. The structure appears airy, with significant gaps between the leaves and stems, allowing light to pass through. This type of shrub is commonly used in urban planters, along sidewalks, or in public parks for decorative purposes.
description: A row of six low, leafy green shrubs with broad, mottled leaves arranged in a straight line, ideal for urban borders or planters.
description_view_0: A narrow, end-on view showing the vertical alignment of the six shrubs, with leaves stacked closely together.
description_view_1: A side view displaying all six shrubs in a straight row, highlighting the broad, rounded leaves and even spacing.
description_view_2: A rear view similar to the front, showing the vertical arrangement and dense foliage from the opposite end.
description_view_3: A side view mirroring view 1, with all six shrubs visible in a line and the leaf pattern clearly displayed.
